name: infernal-forge
summary: A forge in the Nine Hells where damned souls are hammered into weapons and chains. Lava channels replace coal beds; the anvils are big enough for a devil to stand on.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: roughly square or 4:3 — work stations radiate around a central furnace

accent.purpose: lava channels cut through the floor

primary_palette.note: twin forges worked by chained smiths
the forge refines souls, not metal

story_prompts: active — every anvil ringing, souls screaming inside the furnace
a masterwork in progress on the central anvil; the devil-smith has stepped away
abandoned after a cambion uprising, furnace cold, chains still holding skeletons
a shipment is ready — stacks of infernal blades awaiting pickup
sabotage — someone has cooled the central furnace and stolen the key ingot

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: very dim (0.2) with strong orange-red; lava channels and furnace provide dramatic fill

anti_patterns: don't treat it like a blacksmith — this is industrial cruelty, not craft
no coal or wood piles — everything burns on lava or trapped souls
anvils should be oversized (2x2 minimum) — this is devil-scale work
chains are everywhere, but always purposeful (holding, hanging, suspending) — never decorative heaps
